Who's Who in Riemann Hypothesis Research
Home Top 100 North America Europe Asia & Pacific In Memoriam Genealogy Reading List Data Methodology About Corrections Overlap

Researchers across multiple conjectures

People who rank in the directory for more than one of our conjecture sites

The Who's Who project maintains a separate directory for each conjecture: abc conjecture (wwiabc.org), Goldbach conjecture (wwigr.org), Riemann hypothesis (wwirh.org), twin prime conjecture (wwitp.org). This page lists only the researchers who appear in the ranked directory for two or more of those conjectures. For each person it shows how many conjectures they rank on and their rank on each, with a link to that researcher's profile on the relevant site. Because the sites live on separate domains, those links are absolute and will take you to the other site.

The table is sortable. It is ordered first by the number of conjectures a researcher appears on, then by their best (lowest) rank.

Researcher# of conjecturesConjectures and rank (links to each profile)
Igor E. Shparlinski4twin prime conjecture (#5), Goldbach conjecture (#14), abc conjecture (#19), Riemann hypothesis (#53)

A rank shown as #N is that researcher's position on that conjecture's Top 100. A few cross-listed researchers sit just outside a site's Top 100; for those the link goes to that site's Top 100 listing.

Maintained by Steve Hubbard (admin@wwirh.org). Data sources: arXiv, OpenAlex, zbMATH Open, Mathematics Genealogy Project.

Last built June 2026.
